L1.description: A URI validation bypass exists in the SVG processing logic of the PDF rendering engine. When parsing embedded SVG content, element tag names are compared using a case-sensitive check against a protocol whitelist. By supplying mixed-case XML element names in a crafted SVG, an attacker can bypass the URI scheme restriction entirely. This allows loading of arbitrary URL protocols that are normally blocked, which on older PHP runtimes enables arbitrary object deserialization and potential remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The SVG parser uses a case-sensitive string comparison when checking element tag names against a protocol validation whitelist, allowing an attacker to bypass URI validation by using mixed-case tag names and access restricted URL protocols.
